title: cousinia perovskiensis
intro: Cousinia perovskiensis, also known as Cousinia, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Central Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits associated with Cousinia perovskiensis include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a candidate for treating various health conditions, such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Cousinia perovskiensis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
